News & Events
Convocation 2025
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements!
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements! The atmosphere was electric today as we congratulated the brilliant graduates of the 18th Convocation of IST . Your hard work, dedication, and perseverance have finally culminated in this magnificent success. Today, we didn't just award degrees; we launched future leaders, innovators, and change-makers. The entire IST family is immensely proud of each and every one of you. Go forth and conquer! The world awaits your brilliance.
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics"
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics.” The session went beyond theoretical discussion, focusing on how language subtly shapes everyday experiences. It highlighted the ways in which word choices, silences, and patterns of speech reflect underlying structures of power, identity, and gender often in ways that remain unnoticed. The event was particularly enriched by the active engagement of the participants. Their insightful questions, personal reflections, and willingness to reconsider familiar linguistic practices through new perspectives made the session especially meaningful. Such interactions reaffirm Dr. Ausima’s continued commitment to research, teaching, and critical dialogue in the field of sociolinguistics.
Faculty Participation in the Pakistan English Conference - 2025
Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil , lecturers Humanities and Sciences Department, attended the Pakistan English Conference titled “Rethinking English Studies in the Global South”, at Government College University, Lahore on November 27, 2025. During the conference, Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil presented their research paper titled “Teaching Undergraduate Students: A Methodological Shift from Pedagogical to Andragogical Approach” and highlighted the evolving needs of undergraduate learners and emphasized the importance of adopting andragogical strategies to enhance student engagement and learning outcomes. Their participation contributed to the ongoing academic dialogue on the future of English studies in the region and provided valuable representation for the Institute of Space Technology at a national academic forum
2025 IAF Wrold Space Award given to Chang'E6 Lunar Mission including ICUBE-Q
Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards....
Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards. One in individual category and one in team category. In 2023 Elon Musk got the award in individual category and James Webb Space Telescope team (from NASA CSA and ESA) team category. This year 2025 IAF Space Award in team category has been awarded to Chang'E-6 Lunar team that includes all Payloads (including our ICUBE-Q). Award ceremony will be held during annual IAC in Sydney Australia.

Click on link to see details.
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST)
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a....
The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a transformative milestone in Pakistan's journey through space science and technology. As the largest space event in the country, ICAST unites the legacies of IST’s International Conference on Aerospace Science and Engineering (ICASE) and SUPARCO’s International Conference on Space (ICS) into a biennial global forum of unparalleled excellence. ICAST bringing together visionaries, global thought leaders, pioneering researchers, and industry trailblazers, aspires to redefine the future of space science by fostering collaboration, presenting transformative research, and exploring interdisciplinary space applications with the potential to reshape our collective future.

Title: Convocation 2025 – Institute of Space Technology (IST)
Content:
We are proud to announce that the 18th Convocation Ceremony of the Institute of Space Technology (IST) will be held this September to confer degrees upon our graduating class of 2025 . Venue: Jinnah Convention Centre, Islamabad Date: 08th September 2025 For: Undergraduate and Graduate Programs This milestone marks the culmination of years of dedication, innovation, and academic excellence. It is a moment to honor the incredible achievements of our students and the guidance of our esteemed faculty. To all our graduating students — the future scientists, engineers, researchers, and leaders — we can’t wait to celebrate your journey. Stay tuned for updates regarding registration, gown collection, and ceremony details.

Title: Workshop on “Plasma Spray Coatings for Industrial Applications"
Content:
--- Workshop on “Coatings for Industrial Applications ---
Center for Surface Engineering and Tribology, Materials Science and Engineering Department, Institute of Space Technology (IST) with its expertise in materials and surface engineering and corrosion advisory is committed to enlighten the public and private industry of Pakistan by organizing a series of short courses and workshops in specialized engineering areas on a regular basis. These short courses are specially designed to provide a practical understanding of the topics based upon theoretical knowledge of concepts that are involved in the field of Surface engineering through a combination of lectures, lab sessions, and various case studies. This course is highly recommended for professionals dealing with material/coating failures in various fields of engineering in general and personnel dealing with corrosion and tribological analysis in particular. The participation in this short course would help build a pool of qualified professionals with knowledge of the failures generating prudent corrective preventive measures (CPMs).
Workshop on “Plasma Spray Coatings for Industrial Applications"

Title: News & Events
Content:
20 Jan 2021: Director WiSP Lab, Dr. Farrukh Aziz Bhatti recently gave interview to ProUrdu news portal and he discussed about some of the technology fields that are in high demand, the evolving job market due to diffusion of technology and the need for developing skillsets to cope with the changing market needs. He also discussed about the projects being done in WiSP and iVISION labs and the kind of scrutiny done when hiring engineers and researchers for the industry projects being done in these labs. Video Link
07 Oct 2020: Dr. Syed Ali Irtaza has recently returned back from South Korea after completing his PhD from Hanyang University. He delivered a talk in WiSP Lab and discussed his research work about Multi-User Detection Techniques for Grant-Free Access in 5G Massive Machine Type Communications. Massive machine type communications (MMTC) framework targets the wireless connectivity of the autonomous devices in the 5G network. There are many challenges that need to be addressed and much effort is required to completely harness the benefits of 5G MMTC as the behavior of the autonomous devices is quite different form the existing human-centric devices. Therefore, in his talk efficient techniques were introduced to enhance the MMTC communications. One of the proposed technique introduce was to use the pilot and data symbols, and the other techniques include the use of prior information and exploit the inherent structure among the devices’ activity pattern. Finally, computationally efficient algorithms were discussed for the detection of multiusers in 5G network. 30 Jan 2020: Dr. Zaineb Farooq discussed her recently completed PhD work on the topic - Precise localization using Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) . She discussed the concept of positioning using GNSS measurements. The prevalent schemes for precise positioning using GNSS measurements, namely Precise Point Positioning (PPP) and Real Time Kinematic (RTK) were introduced. This was followed by the problem of cycle slips in RTK and ways to mitigate it. Using single frequency GNSS receivers, it was seen that once cycle slips are detected and corresponding measurements taken care of, cm level positioning accuracy can be achieved. Finally, the applications of low cost single frequency receivers in precise positioning were presented. The talk was followed by an interactive discussion on other use cases including indoor localization etc. 06 Jan 2020: Dr. Adeel Razi gave a talk on Neuroscience-inspired Artificial Intelligence . Dr. Razi is an Australian Research Council DECRA Fellow at the Monash University and he is actively collaborating on many computational neuroscience projects with Dr. Sadia Shakil. He explained brain’s anatomy and described how the data gathered non-invasively from the brain is often caused by more than one phenomenon inside the brain. Consequently, the relationship between the brain data and its cause can be one-to-many and more than one mathematical model can fit the data. He then explained the way in which Dynamic Causal Modeling (DCM) is used to find the best models for data fitting. In the next part of his talk, described active inference that is an approach to understanding behavior that rests upon the idea that the brain uses an internal generative model to predict incoming sensory data. He concluded the talk by showcasing recent developments in in-vitro active inference leading towards a proof of principal showing that cultured neurons in a lab can be trained to play a game of pong. The talk was followed by interactive discussion and Q&A session. 02 Nov 2019: Dr. Majid Butt gave a seminar about UE Positioning in a Dense 5G Millimeter Wave Deployment using Supervised Deep Learning . He discussed about some of the key performance metrics of 5G, such as, ultra reliable low latency communication (URLLC) with reliability requirements of >99% and latency requirements of a few milliseconds. He compared conventional positioning indoor positioning schemes with Machine Learning-based positioning while explaining how well can machine learning in a 5G base station (gNB) predict UE positions using available 5G NR CSI report. He then concluded that a combination of serving and neighboring cell beam reports should be used with at least 2 beams coming from the serving cell and at least one from the neighboring cell. The initial results indicate that this approach can give a localization accuracy of a few meters in the indoor environments. The talk was followed by an interactive discussion and QA session. 29 Aug 2019 : EE-14 students Ali Yaseen and Sultan Khan were awarded best final year project Gold Medal at the IST convocation on 29 Aug 2019. The project was titled “Design and formal verification of motion detection using LoRA (Low Range) WAN” and was supervised by Dr. Sobia Jangsher. The project successfully demonstrated indoor localization (without GPS) using LoRA access points. 30 Jan 2019 : A team of researchers from Military College Signals (NUST) visits WiSP Lab for collaboration on Software Radio projects.

Title: News & Advisories
Content:
--- Espionage Campaign to Track Location of Strategic Sites ---
Inspired by Soviet Union’s hybrid warfare techniques, India is using subtle propaganda strategy as well as cyber espionage techniques since it revoked Article 370 to end the special status of Indian-occupied Jammu and Kashmir. One can tune in to 92.7 BIG FM Jammu, while driving from Lahore to Gujrat via GT Road during morning rush hours and learn about the history of Jammu and Kashmir that glamorises the Dogra rule. But now in the present political uncertainty where audio leaks were getting common, Indian cybersecurity company Innefu Labs (with alias Donot Team) released an app on Google Play Store known as nSure Chat which was projected as secure communication platform with next-generation encryption techniques which can’t be broken by the government. Links to this app were sent only to high profile politicians, journalists, scientists, and senior government officials. This app has been now removed from Google play store on numerous reports. More than 3000 individuals were invited to download this app but only around 100 chose to download it. In the aftermath of the unfortunate May 9 incident, there was an increased demand of VPN apps for android to access blocked social media websites. Taking benefit of this opportunity, the same cybersecurity firm released an app on Google Play Store known as iKHfaa VPN under the flag of a fake company called SecurITY Industry. Less than 100 individuals were asked to download this app and around 10 personnel downloaded it. Link to this app was again sent only to high profile Pakistani government officials, scientists and military officials as this was not a general-purpose espionage or ransomware mission. It mines locations of Pakistani individuals even if GPS is turned off (especially unmarked places on Google maps) and reads contact lists as well as messages and chats for confidential info. The same company is also known to host malicious apps outside Play Store at https://mrlockerapp.com which is known to exhibit similar espionage behavior.

Title: News and Activities
Content:
--- IRS Team at TAKRA’26: Showcasing Innovation in RC Aircraft and Avionics ---
We're thrilled to share that our talented IRS team proudly represented our institution at TAKRA'26, held from 12-15 February at University of Central Punjab (UCP). This prestigious event brought together top teams from across the country, providing a platform for students to demonstrate their skills in Remotely Controlled Aircraft Design, Avionics Integration, and Flight Operations. Our team's exceptional performance at TAKRA'26 is a testament to the outstanding quality of our Avionics program, which offers students hands-on experience in: - Aircraft Design and Development - Avionics Systems and Integration - Flight Operations and Testing - Teamwork and Problem-Solving Despite facing tough competition and technical challenges, our students demonstrated remarkable determination, teamwork, and dedication, showcasing their ability to apply theoretical concepts to real-world problems. At IST, we're committed to providing our students with opportunities to push the boundaries of innovation and excel in their chosen field. The experience gained by our students at TAKRA'26 will undoubtedly benefit them throughout their academic and professional careers.
--- Avionics Department in Collaboration with IST Robotics Society Conducted  a Successful 2-Day RC Aircraft Design Workshop ---
Avionics Department, in collaboration with IRS Society, successfully conducted a 2-Day RC Aircraft Design Workshop at Westminster Academy, Islamabad on 16–17 February. The workshop provided students with hands-on exposure to RC aircraft design fundamentals, aerodynamics concepts, and practical building techniques. Participants actively engaged in learning, designing, and understanding the real-world applications of avionics engineering. It was an exciting experience to inspire young minds towards innovation, technology, and aviation.
